title: 2025 12 05 Ida Translations
html:
TL;DR

  • Spanish translation of Project Youth+'s +Account / IDA (Cuenta de Desarrollo Individual) application — Oregon's matched-savings program for residents 12+ pursuing education, vehicle, or emergency-savings goals.
  • Match rate: 5:1 for tangible assets (education, vehicle, fast-track emergency savings). Eligibility caps household assets at $20k (excluding home, primary vehicle, $120k retirement).
  • Administered statewide by Neighborhood Partnerships under the Oregon IDA Initiative; donors get up to 90% state tax credit.
  • 2024–2025 income limits run from $59,300 (1-person household) to $111,750 (8+). Application requires financial education course, banking docs, and an in-person sign-off.
  • Translation work appears to have been Yahir-led — likely a paid or volunteer gig connecting his bilingual skill to PY+'s Grants Pass/southern Oregon outreach.

Notes

This is the full client-facing application: contact info, household members, assets/debts, public benefits (LIHEAP/SNAP/SSI/TANF/WIC/Section 8), income proof, purpose statement, signature page, and the privacy/terms-of-use disclosures. The Spanish phrasing is competent and uses formal "usted" throughout — tone-appropriate for an institutional form for adult applicants.

Why this is in Yahir's vault: PY+ is based in Grants Pass and Yahir's hometown roots + Spanish fluency (Seal of Biliteracy, per his resume) make him an obvious candidate to translate or volunteer-translate the form. This is a paying it forward type of work that connects to his family stability thesis — the +Account program is structurally aimed at exactly the kind of working-immigrant household Yahir grew up in.

Worth flagging: a tax-credit-leveraged matched-savings vehicle for low-income Oregonians is a financial primitive Yahir might surface again as an "if you knew this existed" lever in bridge income thinking.

Connections

  • project youth plus — the program administrator
  • grants pass or — likely service-area context
  • bridge income strategies — the financial primitive class
  • al forthan — adjacent scholarship/financial-aid entity in vault
